← All worlds/World 11
World 11 · 1 state

Hope

Films for the unreasonable conviction that something good is still possible — and the people whose lives become an argument for it.

Choose an emotion
Light After Darkness
On Light After Darkness

You have been in the dark long enough that hope feels like a risk. Better to stay cautious. Better not to believe in things that might not come true. And then something happens — a line in a film, a stranger's act, a moment of ordinary grace — that makes the calculated caution feel less like wisdom and more like fear wearing a coat. These films are not motivational. They are not easy. They are arguments, carefully made, that hope is not naive — it is the most stubborn, evidence-based position available to a person who has been paying attention to how many mornings have arrived anyway.

Light After Darkness

Films for the morning that arrives anyway.

24 films — quick browse

CS9.6
1994

The Shawshank Redemption

Frank Darabont

View Film →

A banker sentenced to life in Shawshank State Prison for a crime he insists he didn’t commit forms a long friendship with a fellow inmate. Hope, as work, across decades.

CS9.0
2020

Soul

Pete Docter

View Film →

A middle-school music teacher who finally lands the gig of his dreams dies on the same day — and finds himself in a celestial waystation, trying to get back.

CS9.0
1997

Life is Beautiful

Roberto Benigni

View Film →

An Italian Jewish father in WWII Tuscany invents an elaborate game to shield his young son from the horror of the concentration camp they are sent to.

CS9.1
1997

Good Will Hunting

Gus Van Sant

View Film →

A self-taught janitor at MIT, secretly a mathematical prodigy, is taken on by a math professor and a therapist who refuse to let him sabotage his future.

CS8.7
2006

Little Miss Sunshine

Dayton & Faris

View Film →

A dysfunctional family piles into a yellow VW van to drive their seven-year-old daughter from Albuquerque to a Californian beauty pageant.

CS8.6
2019

Jojo Rabbit

Taika Waititi

View Film →

A lonely German boy with an imaginary friend version of Hitler discovers his mother is hiding a Jewish girl in their attic.

CS8.4
2013

About Time

Richard Curtis

View Film →

A young man learns the men in his family can travel in time — and slowly discovers the gift isn’t about changing the past but paying attention to the present.

CS9.0
2011

The Intouchables

Nakache & Toledano

View Film →

A young man from the Paris housing estates is hired to be the live-in caregiver to a wealthy quadriplegic aristocrat. An unlikely friendship rebuilds both lives.

CS8.2
2014

Chef

Jon Favreau

View Film →

A high-end LA chef whose career is sabotaged by a critic and a restaurant owner buys a food truck with his son and slowly remembers why he started cooking.

CS8.4
2017

Wonder

Stephen Chbosky

View Film →

A boy born with facial differences attends mainstream school for the first time in fifth grade. The film follows the year from his perspective and the people around him.

CS8.6
1999

The Green Mile

Frank Darabont

View Film →

A death row guard in 1935 meets an inmate with inexplicable healing powers. Based on Stephen King’s serial novel.

CS8.8
1994

Forrest Gump

Robert Zemeckis

View Film →

A man with a low IQ from Alabama lives through fifty years of American history through sheer luck, kindness, and love for one woman.

CS9.5
1993

Schindler's List

Steven Spielberg

View Film →

A German businessman in occupied Kraków uses his factory to save over a thousand Jewish workers from the Holocaust.

CS8.6
2008

Slumdog Millionaire

Danny Boyle

View Film →

A teenager from the Mumbai slums appears on Who Wants to Be a Millionaire and is suspected of cheating. His life story is how he knew the answers.

CS7.6
2000

Pay It Forward

Mimi Leder

View Film →

A seventh-grade boy creates a movement: do something good for three strangers, who each do the same for three more.

CS8.5
2016

Hacksaw Ridge

Mel Gibson

View Film →

Desmond Doss, a Seventh-day Adventist who refused to carry a weapon, served as a combat medic at Okinawa and saved seventy-five lives.

CS8.4
2016

Hidden Figures

Theodore Melfi

View Film →

Three Black women mathematicians at NASA calculate the trajectories for the early space program in 1960s Virginia.

CS9.0
2017

Coco

Lee Unkrich

View Film →

A Mexican boy travels to the Land of the Dead on Día de los Muertos and learns that memory is what keeps love alive.

CS9.0
1997

Life is Beautiful

Roberto Benigni

View Film →

A father turns a concentration camp into a game to protect his young son’s psychological survival.

CS9.0
2011

The Intouchables

Nakache & Toledano

View Film →

A young man from the housing estates is hired as caregiver to a wealthy quadriplegic. An unlikely friendship rebuilds both their lives.

CS8.0
2017

Gifted

Marc Webb

View Film →

A man raises his gifted seven-year-old niece and fights a legal battle against his mother for her.

CS8.4
2017

Wonder

Stephen Chbosky

View Film →

A boy born with facial differences attends mainstream school for the first time in fifth grade. The film follows everyone around him.

CS8.3
2016

Captain Fantastic

Matt Ross

View Film →

A father who raised his children off-grid must navigate the outside world’s expectations.

CS7.8
2015

Eddie the Eagle

Dexter Fletcher

View Film →

Michael Edwards, Britain’s first Olympic ski jumper, finishes last in Calgary 1988 and is beloved for it.

Emotional Growth & Maturity →

For the version of you who finally arrived.

Films don't just entertain. They quietly confirm you were seen — long before anyone in your real life knew how to.

18 emotional states·277 curated films·Growing weekly
